title: Bitcoin crashes to $58K low: Inflation too hot, economy too strong, rate cuts too far?
content:
[Chart bitcoin price crash on server and dollars background]
Artem Burduk/iStock via Getty Images

Just months ago, investors were debating how many rate cuts the Federal Reserve would deliver in 2026. Now, the market is asking a different question: how much lower can Bitcoin go?

Bitcoin (BTC-USD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BTC-USD]) briefly touched ~$58K on Thursday, its lowest level since September 2024, as a mix of hot inflation data, fading rate-cut hopes, and fears around the Strategy (MSTR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MSTR])'s trade sent investors rushing for the exits.

The sell-off came after the U.S. inflation data [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4607201-core-pce-inflation-rises-in-line-with-consensus-in-may-personal-income-spending-exceed-expectations], with the Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) Price Index rising 4.1% YoY in May from 3.8% a month earlier, remaining well above the Federal Reserve's 2% target.

At the same time, the U.S. economy remained resilient, with Q1 GDP growth [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4607502-feds-monetary-stance-is-well-positioned-to-restore-inflation-to-2-ny-feds-williams-says] reportedly coming in at 2.1% versus expectations of 1.6% and jobless claims falling to 215K.

For markets, the message was clear: strong growth, strong consumer spending, a tight labor market, and inflation moving higher again: "This combination gives the Federal Reserve very little reason to cut interest rates anytime soon."

The macro shock triggered massive liquidations across crypto markets. According to CoinGlass, 132.8K traders were liquidated over the last 24 hours, totaling $896.3M, including $424.2M tied to Bitcoin positions alone.

Economist Ted Pillows shared data on X [https://x.com/TedPillows/status/2070237090638389754?s=20] suggesting that selling pressure emerged across U.S., European, and Asian trading sessions.

Some analysts, including Peter Schiff [https://x.com/PeterSchiff/status/2070146847692185798?s=20], argued that the sharp decline in MicroStrategy [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4603360-microstrategys-new-bitcoin-buy-worth-100m-sends-mstr-stock-soaring-7] (MSTR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MSTR]) and its Perpetual Stretch Preferred Stock (STRC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/STRC]) may have triggered a “death spiral” that was putting additional pressure on the world's largest cryptocurrency.

Whether investors agree with that view or not, the market reaction has been brutal. Bitcoin is now down around ~33% YTD and more than ~54% below its all-time highs.

THE PAIN HAS SPREAD ACROSS THE ENTIRE CRYPTO MARKET: 

 	* Ethereum (ETH-USD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ETH-USD]) has fallen more than ~8% this week and currently trades near ~$1,558.4.
 	* XRP (XRP-USD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/XRP-USD]) has dropped over ~10% this week and now sits around ~$1.03.
 	* Solana (SOL-USD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SOL-USD]) has lost nearly ~5% over the same period and trades near ~$66.3.

The Klashi prediction market [https://x.com/Kalshi/status/2070192346851910144?s=20] is increasingly pricing in the possibility of Bitcoin falling toward $45K before the year ends.

For now, the U.S. economy is too strong, and inflation is too high for the Federal Reserve to justify easing monetary policy, and that could keep pressure on crypto markets [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4607479-thune-sees-window-closing-for-crypto-oversight-bill-ahead-of-midterms] in the months ahead.

Some crypto stocks that are mirroring the Bitcoin price crash [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4606902-bitcoin-crashes-but-odds-turn-darker-as-mstr-apld-lead-crypto-stocks-bloodbath] are (COIN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/COIN]), (HOOD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HOOD]), (CRCL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CRCL]), (MTPLF [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MTPLF]), (XYZ [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/XYZ]), (MARA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MARA]), (CLSK [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CLSK]), (BTDR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BTDR]), and (BMNR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BMNR])

MORE ON BITCOIN USD, GRAYSCALE BITCOIN MINI TRUST ETF, ETC.

 	* VanEck Mid-June 2026 Bitcoin ChainCheck [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4916372-vaneck-mid-june-2026-bitcoin-chaincheck]
 	* XRP: Institutional Adoption Is Accelerating Despite The Selloff [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4916003-xrp-institutional-adoption-is-accelerating-despite-the-selloff]
 	* Is Bitcoin Getting Closer To An Uptrend? [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4915948-is-bitcoin-getting-closer-to-an-uptrend]
 	* Thune sees window closing for crypto oversight bill ahead of midterms [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4607479-thune-sees-window-closing-for-crypto-oversight-bill-ahead-of-midterms]
 	* The stock market is overstretched, overpriced – analyst [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4607004-stock-market-is-overstretched-overpriced-analyst]

title: Is Strategy (MSTR) Rebalancing From Bitcoin to AI as Its Next Core Narrative?
content:
Earlier this month, TEOCO Corporation selected Strategy Inc.'s cloud-native Strategy One platform to power its SmartCOGS BillTrak Analysis Module, giving communications service providers self-service, AI-enhanced reporting on wholesale invoices and inventory data. This partnership extends Strategy's AI + BI capabilities into telecom cost management workflows, embedding generative AI agents directly where CSPs make everyday billing and network spend decisions. With Strategy now under pressure to prioritize cash reserves over further Bitcoin accumulation, we'll examine how this shapes its investment narrative.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 38 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10b in market cap - there's still time to get in early.

What Is Strategy's Investment Narrative?

To own Strategy today, you have to believe two things at once: that its Bitcoin‑backed capital structure holds together, and that the underlying AI + BI business quietly keeps earning its place. The TEOCO win for Strategy One fits that second piece: it shows real enterprises are still choosing Strategy's cloud‑native platform to run critical telecom cost analytics, embedding its generative AI directly into day‑to‑day billing and network decisions. On its own this deal is unlikely to move the needle against a US$12,773.70 million loss and intense focus on shoring up US$1.40 billion of reserves, but it does slightly rebalance the near‑term story away from pure Bitcoin leverage toward software relevance. The bigger short‑term catalysts and risks still sit around preferred dividend coverage, capital market access, and whether management keeps prioritizing cash over further large Bitcoin buys.

However, the biggest risk sits where Bitcoin, preferred dividends and dilution all intersect.Despite retreating, Strategy's shares might still be trading 38% above their fair value. Discover the potential downside here.

Exploring Other PerspectivesMSTR 1-Year Stock Price Chart

Seven Simply Wall St Community valuations span roughly US$153 to US$705 per share, underscoring how far apart views sit. Set that against rising preferred dividend obligations and funding stress, and you can see why different investors may reach very different conclusions about Strategy's path from here.

Explore 7 other fair value estimates on Strategy - why the stock might be worth just $152.98!

Reach Your Own Conclusion

Disagree with existing narratives? Extraordinary investment returns rarely come from following the herd, so go with your instincts.

Story Continues

A great starting point for your Strategy research is our analysis highlighting 2 key rewards and 1 important warning sign that could impact your investment decision. Our free Strategy research report provides a comprehensive fundamental analysis summarized in a single visual - the Snowflake - making it easy to evaluate Strategy's overall financial health at a glance.

Contemplating Other Strategies?

These stocks are moving-our analysis flagged them today. Act fast before the price catches up:

Uncover the next big thing with 21 elite penny stocks that balance risk and reward. We've uncovered the 7 dividend fortresses yielding 5%+ that don't just survive market storms, but thrive in them. Capitalize on the AI infrastructure supercycle with our selection of the 49 best 'picks and shovels' of the AI gold rush converting record-breaking demand into massive cash flow.

It does not constitute a recommendation to buy or sell any stock, and does not take account of your objectives, or your financial situation. title: Strategy Faces Pressure as STRC Trades at 17.5% Discount to Par
content:
This article first appeared on GuruFocus.

CryptoQuant's head of research Julio Moreno has suggested that Strategy Inc. (NASDAQ:MSTR) may need to stop buying Bitcoin (BTC-USD) if it wants to rebuild confidence in its shares. Moreno argued that the company's priority should possibly shift toward restoring its dollar cash buffer instead of continuing to buy Bitcoin whenever capital becomes available. The warning comes as Strategy faces pressure from a months-long Bitcoin selloff, with its STRC preferred shares falling to $82.50 last week, a record 17.5% discount to their $100 par value, before trading at an almost 13% discount at Tuesday's close.

Warning! GuruFocus has detected 8 Warning Signs with MSTR. Is MSTR fairly valued? Test your thesis with our free DCF calculator.

Moreno said Strategy needs a more systematic, fundamentals-driven approach to Bitcoin purchases, noting that buying near cycle tops and accumulating during bear markets has contributed to faster unrealized loss growth and weaker STRC fundamentals. Strategy is now sitting on a notional $11 billion loss across its Bitcoin holdings, while its STRC preferred shares pay an 11.5% annual yield that resets monthly and has been raised several times since launch. Based on STRC's current discounted price, the effective yield stands at 13.17%, according to the company's website.

The bigger issue for investors could be whether Strategy can rebuild cash coverage fast enough as its obligations continue to rise. Moreno said the company's dollar cash reserve has fallen 36% since the start of 2026 to $1.4 billion, and that rebuilding it to about $2.8 billion, equal to 24 months of dividend coverage, may be needed for STRC to recover. Still, Strategy bought $34.9 million of Bitcoin between June 15 and June 21, funded entirely through Class A common stock sales, while also using proceeds to increase its reserve by $300 million.

title: MicroStrategy Stock Drops Below $100 For the First Time Since March 2024
content:
Strategy (MSTR), the Bitcoin treasury company led by Michael Saylor, has fallen below $100 for the first time since March 2024, marking a major reversal for one of Wall Street's most popular Bitcoin-linked stocks.

The drop comes as Bitcoin trades near $61,000, highlighting the increasingly tight relationship between Strategy's share price and the cryptocurrency that now dominates its balance sheet.MicroStrategy Stock Performance. Source: Strategy

Bitcoin's Decline Started the Chain Reaction

Strategy owns 847,363 Bitcoin, making it the largest corporate holder of the asset globally. That massive position transformed MSTR from a software stock into a leveraged Bitcoin vehicle.

When Bitcoin surged, MSTR often outperformed. The reverse is now happening.

As Bitcoin retreated from its 2025 highs, the value of MicroStrategy's Bitcoin treasury fell alongside investor appetite for leveraged crypto exposure.

That pressure pushed MSTR through the psychologically important $100 level last tested on March 1, 2024.MicroStrategy's MSTR Performance Since 2024. Source: TradingView

Follow us on X to get the latest news as it happens

The Bigger Problem: Strategy's Funding Engine Is Under Pressure

Bitcoin's decline alone does not explain the magnitude of the selloff.

A key concern is STRC, Strategy's preferred stock vehicle that has been used to help fund Bitcoin purchases. The security was designed to trade near its $100 par value, but recently slipped well below that level.

As STRC fell, its effective yield climbed sharply, increasing Strategy's financing costs and making it harder to raise fresh capital on attractive terms.

For investors, this matters because Strategy's Bitcoin accumulation formula depends on efficient access to capital. When that funding engine weakens, growth expectations weaken too.

The First Bitcoin Sale Shook Investor Confidence

The pressure intensified when Strategy disclosed the sale of 32 Bitcoin to fund preferred stock distributions.

The amount sold was tiny relative to the company's holdings, but the symbolism mattered.

For years, Strategy built its reputation around a near-sacred "never sell Bitcoin" philosophy. Even a small sale challenged that narrative and sparked fresh scrutiny of the company's liquidity needs.

Why MSTR Fell Harder Than Bitcoin

MSTR is not just a Bitcoin proxy. It is a leveraged Bitcoin proxy.

As Bitcoin falls:

Strategy's Bitcoin holdings lose value. Investor confidence in the funding model declines. Premiums to net asset value compress. Financing concerns become more visible.

That combination creates a feedback loop that often causes MSTR to move far more aggressively than Bitcoin itself.

Story Continues

What's Next?

Investors are now watching two critical levels:

Bitcoin's ability to hold the $60,000-$61,000 rangeBitcoin Price Performance. Source: BeInCrypto

Strategy's ability to maintain confidence in its capital-raising model.

If Bitcoin stabilizes, MSTR could rebound sharply given its leverage to the asset. But if Bitcoin remains under pressure and funding concerns persist, the stock's break below $100 may be remembered as more than just a technical milestone.

It could mark a turning point for the Bitcoin treasury trade.

Read the Original story MicroStrategy Stock Drops Below $100 For the First Time Since March 2024 by Lockridge Okoth at beincrypto.com

title: Strategy’s Main Preferred Stock Has a Problem: Retail Investors Are Selling
content:
Strategy’s largest preferred stock issue, STRC, has fallen well below its $100 target price as retail investors sell and concerns grow about how the company funds its dividend payments.

title: Saylor’s bitcoin engine stalled, what’s next for MSTR?
content:
Scott Melker discusses Michael Saylor's bitcoin strategy, the NYSE, Wall Street, stablecoin, and other crypto headlines.

"The Daily Wolf with Scott Melker" airs every day at 12:00 p.m. Tune in for your daily dose of all things crypto.

Make sure to also check out Yahoo Finance's new crypto hub to find the latest crypto-related news.

Video Transcript

0:00 Scott Melker

Michael Saylor's Bitcoin buying machine has sputtered as they aggressively adds to their cash reserve. Meanwhile, the company that owns the New York Stock Exchange is teaming up with a crypto exchange OKX to bring tokenized stocks to 120 million people.

0:23 Scott Melker

And we have announcements from Franklin Templeton and Morgan Stanley. We got a lot to talk about today on the Daily Wolf. Let's go.

0:39 Scott Melker

What is up everybody? Welcome to the Daily Wolf on Yahoo Finance. I am your host, Scott Melker, also known as the Wolf of All Streets. Now we're coming out

0:52 Scott Melker

of a very busy weekend, specifically in the macro. Of course, we had a number of Iran headlines, but we're not going to focus on those today because as you know, we try to separate the signal from the noise and right now, any headline coming out of the war seems to be noise. But what is not noise is deeply analyzing every single week what is happening with one Michael

1:14 Scott Melker

Saylor. So here's the headline as we get every Monday morning.

1:21 Scott Melker

Strategy added 35 million in Bitcoin, 300 million in cash reserves last week. The boost to cash reserves is meant to reassure investors about dividend payments on the company's hard hit preferred shares STRC. So yes, I know that we constantly harp on what is happening here with strategy, but there's a reason because it is the main narrative right now in the market.

1:46 Scott Melker

and just Friday, we talked about the worst day in digital credit history where we saw the massive liquidation event across both CEDA and STRC with STRC trading as low as $82. Now, as you know, STRC is supposed to trade around $100.

2:05 Scott Melker

It's still trading well below par today and as long as it's trading down below 100, it is effectively turned off as far as being able to buy Bitcoin. In fact, they are shutting down the ATM temporarily and not even issuing more shares while it's trading below par.

2:27 Scott Melker

So they bought 520 Bitcoin this week, $34.9 million at an average of 67,068. Shockingly, once again, that's probably the highest price that Bitcoin really traded at this week.

2:44 Scott Melker

They seemed to top take the market. Saylor himself jokes about the fact that he'll be buying the top forever. That seems to be what happened here. The bigger story is that their cash reserve is back up to 1.4 billion. It was at 1.1 billion last week after going as low as around 100 million after they paid 800 million after they paid off that convertible

3:03 Scott Melker

note that caused this massive scare that we've been watching. So, listen, the only way right now that Michael Saylor seemingly has to buy Bitcoin and add to the cash reserve is to sell off Microstrategy stock, which is obviously dilutive, at least temporarily to strategy shareholders. The market is not loving that. They want to see some massive buys and not these small ones. That said,

3:28 Scott Melker

I think if this S, if this uh cash fund gets back up to two, 3 billion over the next few weeks and Bitcoin even slightly rises, this is going to be a narrative of the past. I think another interesting narrative to add to this is that rival Strive, who I've been telling you about with their very attractive product, CEDA, they bought 750 uh nine Bitcoin this week. So they actually bought more Bitcoin this week than strategy was able to buy.

3:58 Scott Melker

So we could see a bit of a transition here from strategy to strive. That has certainly been the trend of late. We're going to see over the next few weeks

4:06 Scott Melker

what happens with strategy's Bitcoin buying machine, but right now, clearly, temporarily, it is sputtering a bit. Now,

4:15 Scott Melker

The next story to me is not going to be the one that grabs the largest headlines, but is arguably the biggest.

4:21 Scott Melker

Let me show it to you here. Intercontinental Exchange and OKX establish joint venture to bridge traditional and digital asset markets. So what they announced today was Ice, which is the parent excha uh parent company of course of the New York Stock Exchange and others. They formed a 50/50 joint venture to build infrastructure for tokenized and digitally native financial products.

4:51 Scott Melker

Now, why is this interesting?

4:54 Scott Melker

So, first of all, they uh did a deal where Nize or Ice, the parent of Nize uh got a board seat and invested in OKX last March. We know that Andrew Cuomo has been working with OKX uh to help with the regulatory and legislative environment.

5:14 Scott Melker

And now this is the huge announcement. And and once again, this is the reason. So, we've seen a lot of crypto exchanges and OKX is one of the largest on the planet. But we've seen a lot of crypto exchanges building products out to attract a new non crypto native audience. Last week we told you about how Coinbase was adding 11 products or so, 9, 10, 11 products all at once to be the everything app.

5:35 Scott Melker

And we've seen a lot of products coming from Wall Street that are trying to bring some Bitcoin into their existing customer's portfolios, right? But this is the first major example where I can see an entity as large as the New York Stock Exchange and Ice, which is arguably the largest entity and institution of its kind,

5:58 Scott Melker

directly trying to create something for a huge swath of crypto native users. OKX has 120 million customers and this is going to bring all of those tokenized products from Wall Street directly to those people.

6:18 Scott Melker

That's why I think that this is so massive and so different. It'll operate as a US uh registered broker dealer and FCM. And so now those 120 million customers from OKX and those people are primarily all around the world. Of course, many of them are US users, but all over the world to access directly tokenized offerings from the New York Stock Exchange. So,

6:40 Scott Melker

this is a completely different direction, a completely different approach, and arguably the one that, in my opinion, is going to have the largest impact on our actual industry.

6:55 Scott Melker

Now I told you about how this isn't just coming in one direction. We also have, of course, the incumbents creating new products to try to give exposure to Bitcoin and crypto in different ways to their existing customer base. We've got two stories about that right now, but the first is this one. Franklin Templeton files first Bitcoin dividend ETFs. Stock income buys Bitcoin automatically. So these are called DRIP ETFs, D R

7:26 Scott Melker

I P. Now, this is an existing structure that's been around for a very long time. It's uh dividend uh reinvestment uh which is what DRIP is, right? And so what this is going to do and they've filed two of them, is effectively, you'll have a basket or an index within the ETF and those of them that have dividends, that dividend will be directly used to purchase Bitcoin into that fund.

7:51 Scott Melker

So it'll be 95% equities, 5% Bitcoin and this is targeted to launch in September. So, this is as I said, not a new product in that there are plenty of these drip ETFs that redirect the dividends back into the asset. So basically, you know, if you have a dividend uh yielding stock, uh you get that dividend and the ETF automatically uses that dividend to buy more of that stock.

8:22 Scott Melker

But this is the first time that it's being used to buy Bitcoin. And this is once again a new and novel product for us that they already exist in the market is an extremely popular. And it shows the maturation of products around Bitcoin and crypto. So, I think that this is absolutely going to be huge. I mean, Franklin Templeton, I believe has 1.3, 1.4 trillion assets under management and will be continuing to aggressively pitch these new products to their customer base.

8:56 Scott Melker

I think it's absolutely huge.

8:58 Scott Melker

On the other side, we have another huge uh entity which is Morgan Stanley. Now Morgan Stanley targets crypto ETF fee crown while Franklin Templeton wants your stock dividends buying Bitcoin. So this is the second half of somewhat of the same story, which is more products being offered to retail in the United States around crypto and Bitcoin. Now, what they're doing here,

9:22 Scott Melker

We saw when Morgan Stanley came into the ETF race a few months ago. I reported on it widely. You can go back and watch those shows. But what was interesting there is that Morgan Stanley generally doesn't issue ETFs, but they had the opportunity to enter a very crowded Bitcoin spot ETF space with their own ETF and their sales force of 15 to 16,000 advisors out there pitching it. Why give the fees to Black Rock when you can create your own product and capture those fees themselves?

9:46 Scott Melker

But it wasn't just about themselves because they came in with the lowest fee product in the market at 14 bips. That is an exceptionally cheap product. It undercut all the existing Bitcoin spot ETFs.

10:05 Scott Melker

And so that was a huge story because it meant that they were also clearly intending to try to compete in the retail space. Now, they're coming in now, this announcement is they've amended their filings for their upcoming Ethereum ETF and their upcoming Solana ETF,

10:28 Scott Melker

both with 14 bip products. So this is the same uh sort of playbook that they recently took with Bitcoin that they're now taking with Ethereum and Solana. They will come in as the cheapest. Uh I believe Eric Eric Bachunis from Bloomberg ETF said that this was going to be the cheapest offerings in the market that he's effectively ever seen.

10:53 Scott Melker

I mean, Wall Street basically spent 10 years calling crypto rat poison. Now they're in a price war war over who gets to serve you that rat poison, right? It's really a just massive change in the way that our industry is viewed and the biggest institutions on the planet are all coming through with a big plan for how to approach it. Now,

11:10 Scott Melker

The next story really leans on two stories that I told you last week. So we have Bank of England soften stable coin rules in final policy draft. So now, you remember last week we had America putting a surveillance camera on on stablecoins, all the big uh regulators and agencies saying that they wanted full KYC and AML on every stable coin. Of course, I told you the story about how

11:32 Scott Melker

Europe, specifically Greece and Lagarde effectively blocked Binance from getting approval and that was a stable coin story as well. Well, now we have a third approach, which is the Bank of England, which was historically the most contentious for stable coins, completely softening their stance and reversing and going the other direction. I mean, the only thing that uh everybody can agree about on stable coins is that they can't agree on how to approach stable coins.

12:02 Scott Melker

Right? But so you now have basically these different regimes. And as I said, the Bank of England was the worst. So when they originally floated their idea for Sterling backed stable coins. That's important. These are not dollar backed stable coins, for Sterling backed stable coins, they proposed that an individual would only be able to hold 20,000 pounds worth of stable coins and a business could only have 10 million. It sent the crypto world into a frenzy. Now, of course, almost all

12:26 Scott Melker

stable coins are dollar backed, very few are Sterling, but what happened here is that people started to say they were going to go to other places to mint stable coins and the Bank of England and the pound would be completely left out. They can't have that happening. So what they've floated now is a 40 billion pound cap on the issuer instead of targeting the individual. This is a complete and utter reversal and it lends to the story that Lagarde was telling in Europe

12:59 Scott Melker

about their fear of dollar stable coins coming in and eating their lunch. I've told you stories like this actually for months now about how a number of governments are fearful of stable coins because why would you use your inferior currency if you are a retail investor in any country, if you can get access to dollars, which is what everybody wants. Well, I'm not sure they can stop this hyper dollarization through stable coins, but they're certainly going to try and that's really what the story

13:30 Scott Melker

here is from the Bank of England. What they're trying to do is make it more popular for somebody to create sterling backed stable coins instead of dollar backed stable coins because they need to somehow compete. But I find it very interesting that the United States has been leading with the genius legislation, but now is calling for higher surveillance.

13:54 Scott Melker

And I told you how they're already doing that through both private companies and through the government having full transparency into your uh into your transactions. Of course, Europe being very contentious now for stable coins and England trying to open the doors. Now,

14:14 Scott Melker

the final story is a string of stories and it's a story I hate telling, but here we are. We had one of the worst weekends uh I can recall, not in size, but just in number of hacks and events across Defy and across crypto. This seems to be a theme that will not die. Now the first was a Microsoft report

14:38 Scott Melker

that they found malware that hijacks crypto wallets and spreads through USB sticks. USB sticks. right? I mean we're in the age of super intelligence of AI, talking about quantum threats, and apparently what's coming for your Bitcoin is a USB stick. Right? I mean we declared this technology obsolete in 2012. I'm not sure the last time I've even put one into my computer and your crypto can survive a 50% drawdown in bear market, but it can't survive Greg from accounting plugging in a mystery thumb drive into his laptop in the parking lot.

15:13 Scott Melker

Right? So we don't know if this has actually been used at all, but it has been, it has been highlighted as a potential risk. The malware is definitely out there and when people plug it in, it effectively infects the computer. You think you're sending a transaction to a friend and instead you're sending it to a hacker. Well, that's not the only story. We've got Ethereum MEV King Jared from Subway, had a massive 50 million exploit. This is ironic because this guy was basically stealing money using MEV bots and then got hacked himself for $50 million.

15:37 Scott Melker

Secret network bridge exploited for 4.7 million with infinite mint bug. and of course Tyco halts its Ethereum layer 2 network after a bridge exploit, token dive. Never even heard of that.

15:48 Scott Melker

Once again, I do this all day every day and these protocols that keep getting exploited are things I have never even heard of or paid attention to it. So, we have a hacking problem. Luckily, uh, right now, it's not the big mainstream names that are getting hacked. I mean, that's really all we've got today. It's interesting to see the approach towards stable coins from uh all these different countries and continents.

16:16 Scott Melker

And of course, interesting to see how institutions are now trying to grab their piece of the crypto pie. We'll be talking about it all week. That's all I got for you today. I'll see you back tomorrow on the next Daily Wolf. Peace.

title: Is Michael Saylor's bitcoin strategy still panning out?
content:
Scott Melker discusses Strategy's (MSTR) move to add $35 million in bitcoin (BTC-USD) and $300 million in cash to its reserves last week, highlighting what this is signaling about the bitcoin trade and current state of the crypto landscape.

"The Daily Wolf with Scott Melker" airs every day at 12:00 p.m. Tune in for your daily dose of all things crypto.

Make sure to also check out Yahoo Finance's new crypto hub to find the latest crypto-related news.

Video Transcript

strategy added 35 million in Bitcoin 300 million in cash reserves last week. The boost to cash reserves is meant to reassure investors about dividend payments on the company's hard-hit preferred shares STRC.

00:15 Speaker A

So, yes, I know that we constantly harp on what is happening here with strategy, but there's a reason, because it is the main narrative right now in the market.

00:26 Speaker A

And just Friday, we talked about the worst day in digital credit history where we saw the massive liquidation event across both Seta and STRC with STRC trading as low as $82.

00:41 Speaker A

Now, as you know, STRC is supposed to trade around $100. It's still trading well below par today.

00:49 Speaker A

And as long as it's trading down below 100, it is effectively turned off as far as being able to buy Bitcoin.

00:57 Speaker A

In fact, they are shutting down the ATM temporarily and not even issuing more shares while it's trading below par.

01:05 Speaker A

So they bought 520 Bitcoin this week, $34.9 million at an average of $67,068.

01:17 Speaker A

shockingly, once again, that's probably the highest price that Bitcoin really traded at this week.

01:23 Speaker A

They seem to top tick the market. Sailor himself jokes about the fact that he'll be buying the top forever. That seems to be what happened here.

01:29 Speaker A

But the bigger story is that their cash reserve is back up to 1.4 billion. It was at 1.1 billion last week after going as low as around 100 million after they paid 800 million after they paid off that convertible note that caused this massive scare that we've been watching.

01:47 Speaker A

So, listen, the only way right now that Michael Sailor seemingly has to buy Bitcoin and add to the cash reserve is to sell off micro strategy stock, which is obviously delutive, at least temporarily to strategy shareholders.

02:06 Speaker A

The market is not loving that. They want to see some massive buys and not these small ones.

02:10 Speaker A

That said, I think if this if this cash fund gets back up to two, three billion dollars over the next few weeks and Bitcoin even slightly rises, this is going to be a narrative of the past.

02:23 Speaker A

I think another interesting narrative to add to this is that rival Strive, who I've been telling you about with their very attractive product, Seta, they bought 750 uh nine Bitcoin this week.

02:40 Speaker A

So they actually bought more Bitcoin this week than strategy was able to buy.

02:46 Speaker A

So we could see a bit of a transition here from strategy to strive. That has certainly been the trend of late.

02:56 Speaker A

We're going to see over the next few weeks what happens with strategy's Bitcoin buying machine, but right now, clearly, temporarily, it is sputtering a bit.

title: Strategy (MSTR) Halts STRC Sales And Sells Bitcoin To Cover Dividends
content:
Get insights on thousands of stocks from the global community of over 7 million individual investors at Simply Wall St.

Strategy's flagship preferred stock (STRC) has traded materially below its US$100 par value, raising fresh concerns about the funding model that supports its dividends. The company has halted new STRC issuances and conducted its first standalone Bitcoin sale since 2022 to help meet preferred dividend obligations. These moves have intensified debate over Strategy's perpetual preferred equity structure, potential dilution, and the role of Bitcoin in its capital planning.

Strategy, which trades as NasdaqGS:MSTR, has built its identity around holding Bitcoin as a core treasury asset alongside its operating business. The recent disruption in its preferred stock funding model comes as industry participants continue to debate how sustainable perpetual equity and high fixed dividend commitments are when tied to volatile digital assets. This episode places renewed attention on how the company balances its Bitcoin exposure with more traditional capital structure tools.

For you as an investor, the key questions now center on how Strategy might adjust its mix of preferred equity, common equity, debt and Bitcoin holdings if platform or market stress persists. Future decisions on dividend policy, further asset sales or new capital raising could reshape both the risk profile of NasdaqGS:MSTR and the relationship between its Bitcoin treasury and shareholder payouts.

Key Considerations

📊 The disruption to STRC funding puts attention on how much of Strategy's capital structure relies on preferred stock and Bitcoin sales to support dividends. 📊 Watch preferred dividends, any further Bitcoin disposals, new equity issuance, and changes in payout policy as signals of how management is managing funding pressure. ⚠️ The most immediate risk is further shareholder dilution, given the recent history of issuance and the need to meet fixed preferred obligations during periods of stress.
